title: [ipv6_addr.c:88-91] lichen_sha256 error handling hides error code details
description:
Gap: The lichen_sha256 call at line 88 returns a negative errno, but the error message only logs %d which doesn't distinguish failure modes.

What: Line 88-91 check lichen_sha256 return value. The error is logged as 'SHA-256 error %d' but lichen_sha256 only returns 0 or -EIO (per lichen_util.c). The caller cannot distinguish between init/update/final failure.

Why it matters: If SHA-256 fails during pubkey-to-IID derivation, debugging requires knowing which stage failed. The conflated error handling in lichen_sha256() (lines 27-30 in lichen_util.c) returns -EIO for any failure. Adding context to the log message or returning more specific errors would improve debuggability.
close_reason: Won't fix P3: lichen_sha256 returning only -EIO is intentional simplification. SHA-256 failure is extremely rare (hardware fault) and distinguishing init/update/final stages provides minimal debug value. The error code is logged. If SHA-256 fails, the system has bigger problems. Refactoring lichen_sha256 for this edge case adds complexity without proportional benefit.
